content.claim: Hungary simultaneously guarantees constitutional protection for institutional and parental educational choice while operating a highly centralized operational system (curriculum, textbooks, staffing centrally controlled), creating a structural governance tension: any reform of the gimnázium entry age would require clarification of which authority locus (Parliament, Klebelsberg Centre, individual maintainers, or church/private entities) controls implementation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: This is identified as a long-standing structural conflict predating the current selection debate; whether it constitutes a legal barrier to reform or merely an administrative complexity is not settled in the notes.
No Constitutional Court ruling is cited that would definitively resolve the autonomy vs. uniformity tension in the context of gimnázium structure reform.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
